title: Maestro Specialist Manifest
description: Formalizes the AGENTS.md S3 specialist manifest. Optional tooling aid: the prose doctrine in AGENTS.md remains the source of truth. A specialist receives exactly this manifest and no conversation history, other tasks, or full plan -- isolation is the advantage.

role.description: What this specialist is (e.g. 'API refactor specialist'). One role per specialist; specialists in a group must differ in role or context, not split identical work (S1 diversity constraint).

task.description: The single scoped objective. Out of scope: report and stop.

files.description: File scope. Anything outside these lists is out of bounds.

read.description: Paths or globs the specialist may read.

modify.description: Paths or globs the specialist may modify. Empty for read-only specialists.

upstream.description: Outputs from prior specialists this task depends on (minimum viable info: a signature, not a 200-line diff).

orientation.description: Cheap structural overview (project map, subsystem index). Navigation aid, not authority -- verify against live code before acting (S7.2).

assumptions.description: Load-bearing assumptions stated up front. If one is uncertain, the specialist must flag it instead of building on it (S7.0).

output.description: Exact deliverable format expected back (e.g. 'changed files + 5-line summary'). Agent prompts must specify max response length (S9 output caps).

accept.description: Acceptance criteria. These are the exit condition, not a post-hoc check (S7.3).

tools.description: Tool scope for this specialist (least privilege).

rules.description: Injected universal rules (S7). In Claude Code these arrive via the system scaffold; listed here for portability.

model.description: Model tier per S9 routing (haiku | sonnet | opus | fable). Cheapest model that handles the task; explicit tier beats accidental frontier inheritance.
